verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' and links to https://developer.copper.co/api-reference/getting-started, but this is simply the Copper API documentation, NOT a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. An app's own API docs do not constitute an MCP server. The flags correctly note 'No official first-party MCP server confirmed; only third-party (ViaSocket, Zapier) and community MCP integrations found.'

The record describes API key auth with HMAC-SHA256 signing using Authorization: ApiKey header + X-Timestamp + X-Signature headers. However, the evidence from developer.copper.co/api-reference/authentication shows this is correct for that endpoint, while developer.copper.com/introduction/authentication.html describes a different token-based approach requiring 'token in the header of every request, along with the email address'. The record appears to only document one variant of API key auth (the HMAC-SHA256 version), missing the simpler email+token approach mentioned in the .com docs.
The copperpricing.com page is unavailable, so we cannot verify the claim that API access requires paid Professional/Business plans. No other evidence documents pricing or plan requirements.
Claims about 14-day free trial and gating to Professional/Business plans cannot be verified since copperpricing.com evidence is unavailable.

Evidence confirms REST API with /platform/ prefix. The breadth='medium' assessment and coverage of CRM entities (people, companies, opportunities, etc.) is reasonable based on the authentication docs and endpoint examples.

The 'buildable-with-caveats' assessment depends on the paid-plan requirement, which cannot be verified since copperpricing.com is unavailable. The HMAC complexity is real but doesn't necessarily warrant caveats vs. buildable-now.

The main_blocker cites paid subscription requirements that cannot be verified (copperpricing.com unavailable). HMAC signing is a complexity but many APIs use it without being classified as having caveats.
Evidence confirms OAuth2.0 Authorization Code Grant flow for partner integrations at developer.copper.com/introduction/oauth/flow.html

revised.name: Copper
revised.one_liner: Copper CRM offers a REST-based Developer API secured by API keys (with HMAC-signed requests) or OAuth 2.0 for partner integrations.

auth_methods.notes: Requires Authorization: ApiKey header plus X-Timestamp and X-Signature (HMAC-SHA256 of timestamp+method+path+body) headers on every request. Generated via System settings > API Keys in the Copper web app.
Authorization Code Grant flow producing a Bearer token; preferred for partner integrations rather than individual users.

revised.self_serve_notes: Pricing page unavailable; unable to verify whether API access requires paid plans or has self-serve free/trial options.

api_surface.notes: Platform API covers CRM entities such as people, companies, opportunities, projects, tasks, and orders. Endpoint structure uses /platform/ prefix; docs include Getting Started and API reference for entities.

evidence.supports: Describes both API Key and OAuth2.0 authentication methods for the Copper Developer API.
Details the required Authorization, X-Signature, and X-Timestamp headers and HMAC-SHA256 signature generation process for API key auth.
Documents OAuth2.0 Authorization Code Grant flow for partner integrations.

flags: No official first-party MCP server confirmed; the URL in original record pointed to API docs, not an MCP server.
Pricing/plan requirements for API access could not be verified (copperpricing.com unavailable).
HMAC request-signing adds implementation complexity beyond typical bearer-token APIs.
